As a result of the 14-mile pursuit, Jennifer was charged with felony counts of second-degree recklessly endangering safety, vehicle fleeing eluding an officer, bail jumping times two, and misdemeanor criminal damage to property. She was subsequently given a $7,500 cash bond.
About two months later, she was found guilty due to a no contest plea for vehicle fleeing eluding an officer. All the other charges were dismissed but read in.
In the end, her sentence was withheld and she was given three years of probation.
